problem_addressed: Efficient computation of temporal betweenness centrality for large temporal graphs where exact methods like Brandes' algorithm become impractical.
core_claim: MANTRA provides a probabilistic framework for high-quality temporal betweenness centrality approximation with guaranteed accuracy and improved efficiency.
key_contributions: Sample-complexity analysis for temporal betweenness centrality approximation
Progressive sampling approach using Monte Carlo Empirical Rademacher Averages
Efficient algorithm for approximating temporal diameter and other graph characteristics
novelty: Extends static graph sampling techniques to temporal graphs and introduces progressive sampling with Rademacher vectors to reduce sample size, while providing theoretical guarantees under all feasible temporal path optimalities.
method_summary: MANTRA uses a two-phase approach: bootstrap phase to estimate parameters (ˆv and ρ(⋆)) and determine initial sample size, followed by progressive sampling with geometric progression and Rademacher vectors to update estimates until desired accuracy is achieved.
method_components: Bootstrap phase with (⋆)-TBFS visits to estimate variance and path length
Estimation phase with progressive sampling and geometric progression
Update mechanism for betweenness estimates, wimpy variances, and Rademacher vectors
key_innovation: Leverages negative correlation among nodes and bounded variance to reduce sample size compared to worst-case VC-dimension bounds, enabling ˜O(log n/ε² · |E|) runtime for characteristic approximations.
assumptions: Maximum variance of estimators bounded by ˆv ∈ (0, 1/4]
Sum of centralities constrained by ρ(⋆) (average internal nodes in paths)
Temporal connectivity rate ζ(⋆) affects approximation quality
theoretical_grounding: Theorem 3 provides sample size bound: |S| ∈ O(ˆv+ε/ε² · ln(ρ(⋆)/δˆv)). Theorem 4 guarantees diameter approximation with error ε/ζ(⋆) using r = Θ(ln n/ε²) samples. Theorem 5 ensures overall ε-approximation with probability ≥ 1-δ.
compute_required: Single laptop (memory measured in GB; ONBRA required >1TB for some datasets)
headline_result: MANTRA outperforms ONBRA in running time, sample size, and memory usage; only feasible option for datasets requiring >1TB memory

results_table.value: lower than ONBRA across all ε
lower than ONBRA across all ε

improvement_over_baseline: MANTRA reduces memory usage by >10× compared to ONBRA (avoids >1TB), requires 2-5× fewer samples, and runs 3-10× faster
unstated_weaknesses: The method is designed for temporal graphs, which may not align naturally with medical image classification tasks
No discussion of how centrality measures would integrate with active learning frameworks
No validation on medical datasets or image-related tasks
No mention of computational requirements or scalability for large medical datasets
No consideration of calibration-aware aspects mentioned in the project topic
threats_to_validity: The effectiveness of centrality measures for active learning in medical imaging is not established
The temporal graph structure may not be the most appropriate representation for medical image data
No evidence that centrality measures correlate with sample uncertainty or informativeness in medical image classification
Potential mismatch between the paper's focus on graph analysis and the project's focus on classification
open_questions: How would temporal betweenness centrality be applied to medical image classification?
What is the relationship between node centrality in temporal graphs and sample selection in active learning?
How would calibration-aware aspects be integrated with this centrality measure?
Can centrality measures effectively identify informative samples for active learning in medical imaging?
Would the computational complexity be feasible for typical medical image datasets?
gaps_this_enables: Could enable new approaches to sample selection in active learning by incorporating temporal centrality measures
Might provide a way to identify important samples based on their position in a temporal graph of data
Could contribute to understanding the relationship between graph structure and learning efficiency in medical imaging
relevance_to_topic: This paper is tangential to the project topic as it focuses on temporal graph centrality rather than active learning or medical image classification.
best_result: MANTRA outperforms ONBRA in running time, sample size, and memory usage; only feasible option for datasets requiring >1TB memory

key_results.Prefix Foremost_running time: lower than ONBRA across all ε
key_results.Prefix Foremost_sample size: lower than ONBRA across all ε

title: MANTRA: Temporal Betweenness Centrality Approximation Through Sampling
